obs_patient_centred.name: Patient-Centred Baseline
obs_patient_centred.description: Best observation prompt — patient-centred framing with clinical reasoning. F1 0.52 with post-processing pipeline (index correction, conclusion stripping, negation filtering)
obs_patient_centred.template:
Extract ALL pathological findings from this radiology report. For each finding, return the core noun (e.g. tumour, nodule, effusion, metastasis, thickening, foci) and its word index. Do not return anatomy, descriptors, or scan parameters.

REPORT (each word is preceded by its index in brackets):
{indexed_report}

EXAMPLE — given a report containing "[5]FDG-avid [6]mass [7]in [8]the [9]right [10]lung. [11]Small [12]effusion [13]noted.", the correct output is:
[{"observation": ["mass", 6]}, {"observation": ["effusion", 12]}]

IMPORTANT: Only extract from numbered findings (1. 2. 3. etc). STOP before any Conclusion: or Comment: section. Each finding noun should appear only once.

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array — include every finding):
[{"observation": ["<word>", index]}, {"observation": ["<word>", index]}, ...]

obs_exp2_inclusion_exclusion.name: Inclusion & Exclusion Lists
obs_exp2_inclusion_exclusion.description: Augmented with valid observation types and terms to exclude. Regression to F1 0.34 — model shifted to list matching
obs_exp2_inclusion_exclusion.template:
Extract observations from this radiology report.

REPORT (as JSON array of [word, index] pairs):
{indexed_report}

OBSERVATIONS are clinical findings - nouns representing abnormalities or medical conditions found in imaging.

Examples of valid observations:
- Tumours: tumour, tumor, mass, lesion, neoplasm
- Nodules: nodule, nodularity
- Fluid/Air: effusion, pneumothorax, ascites
- Consolidation: consolidation, opacity, infiltrate, atelectasis
- Spread: metastasis, metastases, spread, deposit
- Growths: cyst, polyp, abscess
- Inflammation: inflammation, infection, oedema, edema
- Changes: thickening, enlargement, stenosis, obstruction

NOT OBSERVATIONS (do NOT extract):
- Anatomy: lung, liver, spine, bone, lymph node, chest, brain, kidney
- Locations: right, left, upper, lower, medial, lateral
- Descriptors: large, small, mild, moderate, severe, stable, new
- Measurements: mm, cm, SUV, SUVmax
- Negations: no, absent, without, resolved

CRITICAL RULES:
- EXTRACT THE EXACT WORDS as they appear in the text - do not paraphrase or modify them.
- Return each observation as ["word", index] matching the input format.
- observation = the finding itself (noun)

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array):
[{"observation": ["<exact_word>", index]}]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations.

obs_exp3_core_noun.name: Core Noun Selection Guidance
obs_exp3_core_noun.description: Instructs model to prefer clinically specific nouns over generic descriptors. Regression to F1 0.49
obs_exp3_core_noun.template:
Extract observations from this radiology report.

REPORT (as JSON array of [word, index] pairs):
{indexed_report}

EXTRACT each observation with:
- observation: The core finding ONLY (e.g., "tumour", "effusion", "opacity"). Do NOT include location, size, or descriptors.

CRITICAL RULES:
- The report is provided as a JSON array where each element is [word, index].
- EXTRACT THE EXACT WORDS as they appear in the text - do not paraphrase or modify them.
- Return each observation as ["word", index] matching the input format.
- observation = the finding itself (noun)
- Prefer clinically specific nouns: use "consolidation" not "opacity", "metastasis" not "focus"

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array):
[{"observation": ["<exact_word>", index]}]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations.

triplet_v1_baseline.name: Single-Pass Baseline
triplet_v1_baseline.description: Short observation vocabulary, minimal instructions. Hard triplet F1 0.245
triplet_v1_baseline.template:
Extract every pathological finding from this radiology report as a structured triplet.

REPORT (indexed):
{indexed_report}

WHAT IS A FINDING:
- Pathological observations: tumour, mass, nodule, effusion, consolidation, lesion, metastasis, opacity
- NOT findings: normal anatomy (lung, liver), procedures (CT scan), negated findings (no effusion)

For each finding, return:
- observation: the finding noun exactly as it appears in the report, plus its word index
- location: the most specific anatomical site mentioned
- properties: descriptive attributes (size, change status, FDG avidity, morphology)

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array):
[{"observation": ["word", index], "location": "...", "properties": "..."}]

triplet_v2_improved.name: Single-Pass Improved
triplet_v2_improved.description: Expanded vocabulary, location hierarchy, property checklist. Hard triplet F1 0.276
triplet_v2_improved.template:
Extract every pathological finding from this radiology report as a structured triplet.

REPORT (indexed):
{indexed_report}

WHAT IS A FINDING:
- Pathological observations: tumour, mass, nodule, effusion, consolidation, lesion, metastasis, opacity, thickening, collapse, fracture, calculus, hernia, lymphadenopathy, atelectasis, reticulation, cyst, granuloma, gallstones, defect, density, foci, nodularity, plaque
- Include uncertain findings ("possible nodule") -- note uncertainty in properties
- Include stable/unchanged findings
- NOT findings: normal anatomy, procedures, negated findings

For each finding, return:
- observation: the finding noun exactly as it appears, plus its word index from the indexed text
- location: the MOST SPECIFIC anatomical site. Prefer "right lower lobe" over "lung" over "chest". Include laterality and specific structures.
- properties: Check for EACH of these:
  * Size (e.g., "15mm", "3.2 cm")
  * FDG avidity (e.g., "markedly FDG avid", "SUVmax=8.3")
  * Change status (e.g., "new", "stable", "increased")
  * Morphology (e.g., "calcified", "cavitating")
  * Certainty (e.g., "possible", "suspected")
  Use empty string if no properties mentioned.

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array):
[{"observation": ["word", index], "location": "...", "properties": "..."}]

triplet_two_pass_location_props.name: Two-Pass: Location/Properties (Context Window)
triplet_two_pass_location_props.description: Second pass of two-pass pipeline. Given a known observation, extract location and properties from a ±30 word context window. Best configuration: hard triplet F1 0.379
triplet_two_pass_location_props.template:
Given this radiology report, the pathological finding "{obs_word}" was identified at word index {obs_idx}.

REPORT (indexed):
{indexed_text}

For this specific finding, provide:
- location: the MOST SPECIFIC anatomical site. Prefer "right lower lobe" over "lung" over "chest".
- properties: Check for EACH: Size, FDG avidity, Change status, Morphology, Certainty.
  Use empty string if none.

Return ONLY: {"location": "...", "properties": "..."}

default_entity.name: Two-Stage Entity Extraction (Stage 1)
default_entity.description: Extract clinical entities from radiology reports - first stage of two-stage pipeline
default_entity.template:
You are a clinical NLP system extracting entities from radiology reports following the {schema_name} schema.

ENTITY TYPES:
{entity_types}

TASK: Extract all clinical entities from the report below. For each entity, identify:
- text: The exact word(s) from the report
- type: One of the entity types listed above
- start_word: The word index where the entity starts (0-based)
- end_word: The word index where the entity ends (0-based, inclusive)

GUIDELINES:
- Extract observations (clinical findings like tumour, effusion, nodule)
- Extract anatomical structures (lung, liver, pleura, spine)
- Extract modifiers that describe observations (large, stable, new, FDG-avid)
- Be comprehensive - extract ALL relevant entities
- Use exact text as it appears in the report

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON array):
[
  {{"text": "tumour", "type": "OBS", "start_word": 5, "end_word": 5}},
  {{"text": "left lung", "type": "ANAT", "start_word": 8, "end_word": 9}},
  {{"text": "large", "type": "OBS-MOD", "start_word": 4, "end_word": 4}}
]

Extract entities only. Do NOT identify relations - that will be done in a second step.

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations.

indexed_entity.name: Indexed Entity Extraction
indexed_entity.description: Entity extraction with indexed report text for precise word matching
indexed_entity.template:
Extract clinical entities from this radiology report.

ENTITY TYPES:
{entity_types}

REPORT (each word prefixed with its index [N]):
{indexed_report}

CRITICAL RULES:
1. SEPARATE observations from anatomy. "hepatic foci" = TWO entities: "foci" (Observation) + "hepatic" (Anatomy). "bone metastases" = "metastases" (Observation) + "bone" (Anatomy).
2. Observations are finding NOUNS ONLY: tumour, mass, nodule, effusion, metastasis, thickening, lesion, foci, lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly, adenoma, mastectomy, collapse, stenosis.
3. Anatomy is body parts: lung, liver, colon, bone, lobe, segment, pericolic, hepatic, mediastinal, cervical, supraclavicular, pleural, skeletal.
4. Multi-word anatomy is OK: "left adrenal gland", "right upper lobe", "segment VII".
5. Use Observation:Uncertain for: suspicious, possible, indeterminate, concerning, may represent.
6. Use Observation:Definitely Absent for: resolved, no [finding], absent, negative.
7. Get word indices from the [N] prefix. Each entity appears ONCE.

EXAMPLE — "[5]FDG-avid [6]mass [7]in [8]the [9]right [10]lung. [11]No [12]effusion.":
[{{"text": "mass", "type": "Observation:Definitely Present", "start_word": 6, "end_word": 6}}, {{"text": "right lung", "type": "Anatomy", "start_word": 9, "end_word": 10}}, {{"text": "effusion", "type": "Observation:Definitely Absent", "start_word": 12, "end_word": 12}}]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array.

default_relation.name: Two-Stage Relation Extraction (Stage 2)
default_relation.description: Extract relations between entities - second stage of two-stage pipeline
default_relation.template:
Link observations to their anatomical locations in this radiology report.

ENTITIES:
{entities_list}

RULES:
1. ONLY use located_at: links an Observation to the Anatomy where it was found.
2. ONLY link entities that appear in the SAME finding (same numbered item 1. 2. 3.).
3. Each observation should link to at most ONE anatomy (the most specific one).
4. Use suggestive_of ONLY when the report explicitly says "suspicious for" or "suggestive of".
5. Do NOT create modify relations.
6. Be conservative — fewer correct relations are better than many wrong ones.

EXAMPLE — given entities: e1="mass" (Observation), e2="right lung" (Anatomy), e3="effusion" (Observation), e4="pleural" (Anatomy):
[{{"entity1_id": "e1", "entity2_id": "e2", "relation_type": "located_at"}}, {{"entity1_id": "e3", "entity2_id": "e4", "relation_type": "located_at"}}]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array.
